description: GTO bet sizing theory by street and situation for common stack depths

100bb.rationale: Standard open at deep stacks; smaller from later positions

50bb.rationale: Slightly smaller opens at medium stacks reduce exposure

25bb.rationale: Shallower stack demands tighter opens with smaller sizing

oop.rationale: OOP needs larger sizing to reduce opponent's positional edge

4bet.rationale: Below 25bb effective, 4bet = shove with committed stack

small_cbet.use_when: Range advantage on dry boards; high frequency

large_cbet.use_when: Wet boards; need to charge draws; lower frequency

standard.rationale: Geometric sizing to set up river shove

probe_bet.use_when: IP after OOP checks flop; pick up dead money

overbet.use_when: Nut advantage, polarized range, deep stack

value_bet.rationale: Standard value sizing; price in drawing misses

block_bet.use_when: Thin value or pot control; induce bluff-raises

overbet_shove.use_when: Nut hands; maximize value vs. strong calling ranges

geometric_sizing.concept: Size bets so that betting flop-turn-river puts you all-in by river
geometric_sizing.formula: Each street bet = (remaining stack) / (streets remaining + 1) as a percentage
geometric_sizing.example_50bb: Pot 6bb preflop → Flop 3bb (50%) → Turn 7bb (58%) → River all-in
